trivia_bullets.text: Throne of Eldraine (set code ELD, released 2019-10-04) is a fairy-tale-inspired set set on the plane of Eldraine, divided between the knightly Realm (five courts: Ardenvale, Vantress, Locthwain, Garenbrig, Embereth) and the magical wilds inhabited by the Fae. Ardenvale is the white-aligned court of loyalty and knighthood. [Scryfall], [magic.wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Eldraine]

Flavor text: "As the blaze raced toward her, Syr Ameril closed her eyes and pictured Castle Ardenvale so her last thoughts would be of home." Syr Ameril is not a named character with an independent card — the title 'Syr' is the honorific for knights of the Realm in Eldraine. The vignette casts Ardenvale as a place of deep knightly belonging, invoked at the moment of death. [Scryfall]

Castle Ardenvale is a real card in the same set (ELD, card no. 238) — a land that produces white mana and can create 1/1 Human creature tokens. The flavor text of Scorching Dragonfire names it as a knight's last thought of home, making the two cards a micro-narrative pair within the set. [Scryfall: Castle Ardenvale ELD/238]

The exile clause ('if that creature would die this turn, exile it instead') was a design choice to prevent recursive graveyard-based decks from getting value off the destroyed creature — relevant in the ELD Standard environment that included Cauldron Familiar and other sacrifice-value loops. EDHREC rank 11,946 — moderate commander play. [Scryfall]

related_cards.name: Castle Ardenvale
related_cards.note: Named in flavor text as the dying knight's image of home; direct narrative pair

related_cards.name: Syr Gwyn, Hero of Ashvale
